tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Laboratory results show good glycemic control and normal renal function. Electrolytes and most metabolic parameters are within normal limits, except for mildly decreased total CO₂, which may suggest mild metabolic acidosis. Lipid profile reveals elevated total and LDL cholesterol, and low HDL cholesterol, indicating increased cardiovascular risk. Urine microalbumin/creatinine ratio is normal. Recommend addressing dyslipidemia and monitoring acid-base status as clinically indicated.

display.value: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
